race_name: L'Etape Norway by Tour de France

top_categories.workout_context: L'Etape Norway by Tour de France's rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 80 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Etape Norway by Tour de France's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before June.
61ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At L'Etape Norway by Tour de France, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
When someone attacks on rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Etape Norway by Tour de France. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 80, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Varied gradients on rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced demand climbing versatility. Sit the shallow stuff, stand the steep stuff, switch without thinking.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At L'Etape Norway by Tour de France, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 80 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 4,921ft of climbing on rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for L'Etape Norway by Tour de France’s 80 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at L'Etape Norway by Tour de France. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at L'Etape Norway by Tour de France requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced landscape to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 80 miles of rolling to mountainous with fjord-influenced landscape in Trondheim, Trøndelag, Norway.
